Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for recent emails sent to javier.morales@moveops.com using search_emails (query: "Sasha" or "Jordan" or "Rachel" or "Nashville" or "Denver" or "Portland" or similar). The agent needs to find four emails: (1) Sam Delgado (sam@pivotpointlabs.com) requesting Sasha Greenwald's Nashville move be pushed up from April 18–20 to April 11–13 due to a client kickoff on April 14, (2) Sasha Greenwald (sasha.greenwald@pivotpointlabs.com) confirming April 11 works and revealing she has a cat (~10 lbs, named Mochi) requiring pet-friendly housing that was not mentioned during intake, (3) Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) requesting Jordan Whitfield's apartment be changed from 1BR to 2BR because his partner is joining him in Denver, and (4) Patricia Langford (patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io) checking on Rachel Dominguez's Portland move status and asking if the apartment near MAX light rail is confirmed.
- Search Slack #operations channel using conversations_search_messages (query: "Heartland" or "vendor" or "payment hold" or similar). The agent needs to find Chloe Vance's announcement that Heartland Movers is on payment hold due to $4,800 Q1 overbilling for cancelled and misattributed moves, with instructions to use Swift Relocations (contact: Greg Pallone) for any new bookings. The agent also needs to find Chloe's follow-up clarifying the hold only affects new bookings, not existing confirmed moves.
- Search Slack #operations channel using conversations_search_messages (query: "Nashville" or "UrbanNest" or "apartment" or similar). The agent needs to find Blessing Okafor's warning that Nashville furnished housing availability is extremely tight through mid-April due to a music industry conference, and that pet-friendly options are basically zero.
- Search Airtable Relocations table using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (query: "Sasha Greenwald" or similar). The agent needs to find the current record showing PivotPoint Labs, NYC to Nashville, In Progress, April 18–20, 1BR/studio with natural light, walking distance to coffee shops, equipment stipend $1,900, with no pet requirement listed.
- Search Airtable Relocations table using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (query: "Jordan Whitfield" or similar). The agent needs to find the current record showing BrightLoop Analytics, Minneapolis to Denver, In Progress, April 21–23, 1BR furnished near downtown, economy flight, equipment stipend $1,800.
- Search Airtable Relocations table using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (query: "Rachel Dominguez" or similar). The agent needs to find the current record showing Canopy Health, LA to Portland, In Progress, April 7–9, 1BR furnished near public transit, equipment stipend $2,000.
- Search Linear for existing tickets using linear_list_issues or linear_search_issues (query: "Whitfield" or "apartment" or "Denver" or similar). The agent needs to find the existing ticket "Apartment sourcing, Jordan Whitfield (BrightLoop, Denver)" which still shows 1BR specs and 3 shortlisted 1BR units from UrbanNest, needs updating to 2BR.
- Look up email addresses for client contacts using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts (query: "Sam Delgado" or "Tessa Moreno" or "Patricia Langford" or "Chloe Vance" or similar). The agent needs these to send confirmation emails and flag Chloe.
- Update Sasha Greenwald's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records: change Move Start Date to 2026-04-11, Move End Date to 2026-04-13, and add to Special Requirements that she has an indoor cat (~10 lbs) requiring pet-friendly housing.
- Update Jordan Whitfield's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records: change apartment requirement from 1BR to 2BR in Special Requirements (partner joining).
- Update the existing Linear ticket for Jordan Whitfield's Denver apartment sourcing using linear_update_issue (or similar): change description to reflect 2BR requirement instead of 1BR, note partner is joining.
- Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue (or similar) for Sasha Greenwald's Nashville apartment sourcing with urgent priority, noting the accelerated April 11–13 timeline, pet-friendly requirement (indoor cat), and tight Nashville market due to the music conference.
- Send an email using send_email (recipients: ["sam@pivotpointlabs.com"], subject and content confirming the date change to April 11–13 and flagging that Nashville housing availability is tight due to a conference).
- Send an email using send_email (recipients: ["sasha.greenwald@pivotpointlabs.com"], subject and content confirming the accelerated move dates and acknowledging her cat will be accommodated with pet-friendly housing).
- Send an email using send_email (recipients: ["tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com"], subject and content confirming the 2BR apartment change for Jordan and his partner).
- Send an email using send_email (recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io"], subject and content confirming Rachel Dominguez's Portland move is on track for April 7–9).
- Send a message using conversations_add_message (channel_id targeting #operations or Chloe Vance) flagging that Sasha Greenwald's Nashville move cannot use Heartland Movers due to the payment hold and needs to be booked with Swift Relocations, noting the accelerated timeline and Nashville housing crunch.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or similar) to look up relocation records.
tool selection

✓Run #3The agent called airtable_search_records for all three employees (Sasha Greenwald, Jordan Whitfield, Rachel Dominguez) using the correct base_id and table_name 'Relocations'.

4 When querying Airtable for relocation records, the model must target the Relocations table (tblRelocations01) and pass employee names (e.g., 'Sasha Greenwald', 'Jordan Whitfield', 'Rachel Dominguez') as the search value.
